name: Big Batch Lemon Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Oil and lightly flour muffin pans.
3. Sift all-purpose flour, cream of tartar, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg into a large bowl. Stir in whole wheat flour and wheat germ. Set aside.
4. Place tofu and 2 tablespoons lemon juice in a food processor and blend until smooth. Set aside.
5. In a small bowl, whisk egg replacer with water until foamy.
6. In a medium-size bowl, mix applesauce, brown sugar, corn syrup, and juice concentrate. Stir in egg replacer, vanilla, lemon peel, and remaining 2 tablespoons lemon juice.
7. Add the applesauce mixture to the dry ingredients alternately with the tofu mixture, stirring after each addition.
8. Fill muffin cups 2/3 full with batter and lightly dust the surface of each with cinnamon.
9. Bake until muffins are light brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 18 minutes.
10. Turn out onto a wire rack to cool. Serve warm or wrap and store at room temperature when cool.
Per muffin: 143 cal, 5 g prot, 100 mg sod, 29 g carb, 2 g fat, 0 mg chol, 83 mg calcium
From: Marie Oser, in Vegetarian Gourmet (Winter 1993)
Typed for you by Karen Mintzias
